requirements.requirement: CIS Core remains the single authoritative constitutional specification
Companion specifications inherit Core terminology rather than redefining it
Every artifact carries consistent governance status, stewardship, versioning, traceability, and release lifecycle fields
Implementation profiles adapt CIS for domain-specific constraints without changing constitutional requirements
Standards traceability answers requirement, component, evidence, replay, receipt, and test for each capability
Research OS preserves evidence, insights, ideas, and actions as durable institutional memory
Major artifacts follow the shared governance model
External standards families are mapped for interoperability and traceability
The conformance suite is generated from the traceability matrix source of truth
CORI Alpha is the first independently verifiable constitutional proof
tests: tests/release/cori-alpha-proof.test.ts replay, receipt, and conformance coverage

validationFamilies.name: Constitutional Verification
validationFamilies.purpose: Verify that each requirement traces through the full constitutional path.
acceptanceCriteria: Every requirement maps to Architecture, Ontology, Knowledge Graph, GKS, Research OS, Reference Runtime, Conformance, Evidence, Replay, and External Standards.
Every requirement has a stable component and evidence surface.
No requirement invents a new constitutional obligation.

validationFamilies.name: Replay Validation
validationFamilies.purpose: Verify that the evidence package can be reconstructed deterministically from the recorded replay path.
acceptanceCriteria: Replay produces the recorded evidence package.
Replay does not depend on private explanation or hidden state.
Replay confirms the governing decision record.

validationFamilies.name: Receipt Validation
validationFamilies.purpose: Verify that the constitutional receipt set is complete and consistent with the governing record.
acceptanceCriteria: Each receipt matches the governing decision record.
No receipt contradicts the evidence package.

validationFamilies.name: Trust Validation
validationFamilies.purpose: Verify canonical state, stewardship, and trust ledger consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Trust ledger entries agree with canonical transitions.

validationFamilies.name: Acceptance Criteria
validationFamilies.purpose: Verify that the suite records an objective pass, partial, or fail outcome.
acceptanceCriteria: No blocking requirement remains unverified for pass.
Subjective readiness claims do not override evidence.
The suite remains synchronized with the traceability matrix.
The suite input remains machine-readable and replayable.
The suite produces evidence-first outcomes only.
The suite identifies blocking gaps instead of overstating readiness.
